text: Becky Biberstein Michael Green Frances Carr Tom Richhart Troy Cook Bobbie Jackson Kimberly Abney Sydnee Bradburn Lean ne Lee Jeff Tillman Mike Holland and Matt Norris Thirteenth Sunday in Ordinary Time June 28 2026 This weekend June 27th 28th the Church celebrates the Thirteenth Sunday in Ordinary Time In addition to this celebration our local Church is celebrating the sixtieth anniversary of the dedication of our church building Liturgically this celebration is considered a solemnity in our parish meaning that we are able to celebrate it this Sunday with the joyful liturgical color of whitegold as the rest of the world dons the hopeful green of Ordinary Time On June 26 1966 Fr Bob Traub our eighth resident pastor welcomed Bishop Leo Pursley the sixth bishop of our diocese to dedicate our new church building By all accounts it was a sweltering Sunday as temperatures swelled to a high of 93F Yet the blazing heat didn't keep parishioners and various guests from attending as the newly built church was overflowing by the time that the Mass of dedication started Though we still hear the readings for the Thirteenth Sunday in Ordinary Time today the prayers of our Mass will come from the Common of the Dedication of a Church on the Anniversary of the Dedication In these prayers we give thanks to God for this building a visible sign of the Church herself It is in this visible house that God let us build that He wonderfully manifests and accomplishes the mystery of His communion with us Every year around this time I also like to reflect on the words that Fr Traub shared in the fundraising efforts for the new church in the early 1960s This building will serve as a testimony to our story the vibrant epic of the way in which the life of God is nourished in our souls It begins with the first blessing sought by the expectant mother for her newly conceived child and ends only with the last benediction begged by grieving families for their departed loved ones During all the years in between filled with work and rest joy and sorrow pleasure and pain the Altar is the center of our attention Our freely offered sacrifices at this time will be recognition of the value we place upon our supernatural life and our desire for it to grow stronger in St Joseph Parish And every year these words strike me as how true and beautiful they are even more so this year as we renovate the church building itself Parish Council Nominations Please return any remaining nominations for the Parish Council either to the Parish Office or in the collection basket by next weekend Once nominations are closed those nominated will be contacted in July to ensure that they are willing to accept the nomination before two new names are drawn to join our Parish Council Nomination sheets are available in the Narthex Parish Renovation Updates Over the past two weeks Lima Pipe Organ has been tirelessly reinstalling our pipe organ This is the first major rebuild of our pipe organ since it was installed sixty years ago As a true pipe organ the organ is played by forcing air from bellows through physical pipes of varying lengths each producing a different note The majority of this system is behind the screen on the back Church wall and was in need of extensive repair and tuning Though the organ is now playable there is still another few days of work to complete the reinstallation and for final tuning Once the Church renovation is complete we've invited Parker Maas the owner of Lima Pipe Organ and a master organist himself to offer an organ concert to show off our organ's full range Watch for a date for this future event sometime this Fall I say sometime this Fall as Shawnee Construction has once again needed to push our project end date slightly down the road now to mid August In good news the marbling flooring for the sanctuary should be arriving in the next two weeks In not so good news the millwork from Big Lumber continues to be delayed As of our last progress meeting this past Friday the last of our millwork package is now scheduled to arrive by July 17th Saturday June 27 8400 4:45pm Confession 5:00pm Jim Timmerman Sunday Harris Volunteer 10:00am Ellie Coyne Sam Smith JTWyatt Davis The Parish Office is closed on Friday July 3rd for the Fourth of July Holiday Parish Renovation Updates Cont Though the continued delays can be frustrating all those who have ever done construction work on their homes knows it is to be expected Thankfully with these delays we have received renewed assurances from Shawnee Construction that they will use this extra time to ensure that they are providing only the best quality work for our parish Thank you for your continued patience support and prayers as we bring our renovation work closer and closer to completion In Christ Fr David Women of St Joseph do you feel called to join with others in your prayer for our parish priests Consider joining the Seven Sisters Apostolate To participate you would commit to praying one Holy Hour for our parish priest Fr David on ONE DAY OF THE WEEK of your choice Ideally this Holy Hour would be done in front of the Blessed Sacrament in the Church when possible Through the Seven Sisters Apostolate we will ensure that a Holy Hour is prayed each day of the week for our parish priest's benefit To join this apostolate or for more information
